Search

Real-time web search with source citations. The feature that taught us CI is not your laptop.

How it works

When you select Search mode, your message goes through a search pipeline. The pipeline routes intent, executes the search, and formats results with source URLs. The model then answers using the search results as context.

The search progress indicator shows while the search is running. It disappears when the results come back.

What broke

Problem Root cause Fix
Progress indicator not found in E2E tests search_progress was not visible within 5 seconds on slow CI. The element existed but was hidden or not rendered yet. Extended wait to 10 seconds. Added a dynamic wait_for that checks if the element exists before waiting for it to be visible.
Search returned empty results The search API timed out on CI. The backend returned an empty array instead of an error. Added a timeout check in the search pipeline. Return a meaningful error instead of empty results.
Citations not rendering The search results included URLs but the Markdown renderer did not auto-link them. Wrapped URLs in Markdown link syntax before passing to the renderer.

The CI lesson

On a MacBook Pro, the search progress indicator appears in under a second. On GitHub Actions with a Ubuntu runner, it takes 5 to 8 seconds. The E2E tests assumed the MacBook Pro timing.

The fix was not just waiting longer. It was checking whether the element exists at all before waiting for it to be visible. If the element does not exist, the test should fail with a clear message, not hang for 10 seconds.

# Before: always waits 5 seconds
page.wait_for_selector(".search-progress", state="visible", timeout=5000)

# After: checks existence first, then waits up to 10 seconds
if page.locator(".search-progress").count() > 0:
    page.locator(".search-progress").first.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)

The macOS app

The Swift app does not have search mode yet. It sends messages through the GenerationAPI which calls /api/generate. The search pipeline lives in the backend and is triggered by the web frontend when Search mode is selected.

Found something unexpected? Report an issue.
